help.title: Help Center
help.subtitle: How can we help you today?

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do I download my purchased patterns?
faq.a1: After purchase, you can find all your patterns in the 'My Profile > Order History' section. You can download the PDF files directly from there.
faq.q2: Can I sell the patterns I create?
faq.a2: Absolutely! You can register as a creator and list your patterns on our Marketplace. Go to the Editor and click 'Publish' to get started.
faq.q3: How does the AI Generator work?
faq.a3: Our AI analyzes your text description or uploaded image to generate a unique knitting chart. You can then edit it in our Pattern Editor.
faq.q4: What file formats can I export for designs created in the Pattern Editor?
faq.a4: You can export your patterns as PDF (including instructions) or PNG images, and your project is saved securely on byKnit so you can edit it at any time in the Pattern Editor.

contact.title: Still need help?
contact.desc: If you couldn't find the answer you were looking for, feel free to contact us via email.

sections.rights: Rights of Users and Legal Representatives

content.overview: 84Company (hereinafter 'Company') values your personal information and complies with Article 30 of the 'Personal Information Protection Act', related laws, and GDPR (EU General Data Protection Regulation) regulations. This policy discloses what information we collect and how we use it.

collectedItems.required: Required: Email, Password, Nickname, Purchase/Payment records
collectedItems.optional: Optional: Profile image, knitting proficiency and preferences
collectedItems.auto: Auto-collected: IP address, cookies, service usage records, device info

content.purpose: The company processes personal information for service provision, identification, personalized recommendations, development of new services, dispute resolution, and civil complaint handling.
content.retentionPeriod: In principle, we retain information while you use the service and destroy it immediately upon account withdrawal. However, records required by relevant laws (Commercial Act, Electronic Commerce Act, etc.) will be stored for the prescribed period.
content.destruction: The company destroys information without delay upon achievement of purpose. Electronic files are destroyed using technical methods that prevent recovery (permanent deletion), and paper documents are shredded.

outsourcing.title: To improve service, we entrust processing to professional companies as follows:
outsourcing.vercel: Vercel: Web hosting and infrastructure management
outsourcing.google: Google LLC: Platform analytics (GA4) and ad optimization

content.overseas: We use cloud services (Supabase, Vercel) for efficiency and stability, and in this process, personal information may be stored or processed on overseas servers (e.g., AWS).
content.rights:
1. Data subjects may exercise the following personal information protection rights towards the Company at any time:
   A. Right to request access to personal information
   B. Right to request correction in case of errors, etc.
   C. Right to request deletion
   D. Right to request suspension of processing (Including Do Not Sell/Share My Personal Information)

2. Right to Opt-out of Selling or Sharing Personal Information:
   The Company does not sell (transfer for value) the user's personal information to third parties. If the Company sells or provides personal information to third parties for advertising purposes in the future, users can exercise their right to opt-out at any time.
   * How to Opt-out: Contact the customer center (vbxn4321@gmail.com) via email or configure 'Withdraw consent for third-party provision' in the 'My Account' page.

3. Rights may be exercised through writing, email, FAX, etc., and the Company will take measures without delay.

cookies.title: Users have the right to choose cookie installation.
cookies.chrome: Chrome: Settings > Privacy and security > Clear browsing data or Cookie settings
cookies.safari: Safari: Settings > Privacy > Block all cookies
cookies.edge: Edge: Settings > Cookies and site permissions

content.safety: In addition to technical measures such as SSL encryption and one-way password encryption, the company performs administrative measures such as 'establishment and implementation of internal management plans' and 'regular privacy training'. Connection records are also kept and inspected for at least one year as required by law.
content.automatedDecision: While the company utilizes AI technology to provide services, it does not make 'automated decisions' that significantly affect the rights or obligations of users. If such a system is introduced in the future, data subjects will be notified in advance, and they will be able to exercise their right to refuse and request an explanation.

remedies.title: For other infringement reports or consultations, please contact the following agencies:

cookieBanner.message: We use cookies to improve your experience and for marketing purposes.

content.art1: These terms and conditions aim to define the rights, obligations, and responsibilities of the Company and users in using electronic commerce-related services provided by the pattern and handmade platform "ByKnit" operated by 84Company.
content.art2:
1. "ByKnit" refers to a virtual place of business established by the Company to trade patterns and related products among members.
2. "Member" refers to a person who has registered for membership by accessing ByKnit.
3. "Seller" refers to a member who registers and sells digital content such as patterns or products.
4. "Buyer" refers to a member who purchases products from a seller.
5. "Mail-order Brokerage" refers to providing a platform where transactions among members occur. The Company, as a mail-order broker, is not a party to the transaction and is not responsible for information or transactions registered by the seller.
content.art3:
1. When the Company revises the terms, it shall announce the effective date and reasons for revision on the initial screen of the service from 7 days before the effective date to the day before.
2. [Anti-Dark Pattern Policy]: For changes that significantly affect users' rights, such as price increases for paid services or automatic payment conversion, the Company must notify members individually at least 30 days in advance and obtain explicit consent.
content.art6: The Company strives to protect members' personal information and follows a separate 'Privacy Policy' for collection and use.
content.art10:
1. Sellers are responsible for the quality and information of registered patterns and products.
2. The Company may provide a payment protection system (escrow) for safe transactions.
content.art16:
1. [Specifics of Digital Content]: Digital content such as PDFs, in accordance with Article 17, Paragraph 2, Item 5 of the 'Electronic Commerce Act', cannot be withdrawn or refunded due to a simple change of mind once download has started or viewing is complete.
2. However, refunds are possible if the provided file is significantly different from the description, contains serious errors, or in case of duplicate payments.
3. Refund requests must be submitted within 7 days of purchase through the customer center.
content.art20:
1. Copyright for postings like reviews and pattern images posted by members belongs to the member.
2. [Usage License]: The Company has a non-exclusive license to use (reproduce, distribute, transmit, create derivative works) member postings worldwide for free for purposes of service operation, promotion, exhibition, and sales promotion.
content.art22:
1. Members shall not unauthorizedly distribute, share, or resell paid patterns, infringe on others' copyrights, or write malicious comments.
2. Upon detection, the Company may suspend service, delete postings, permanently terminate membership, and take legal action after prior notice (immediately in emergencies).
content.art28:
1. The Company only provides an intermediary platform and does not guarantee the truthfulness or quality of product information registered by sellers.
2. The Company is not liable for inability to provide services due to force majeure.
content.provision: These terms and conditions shall take effect from January 22, 2026.

calculator.title: Knitting Math Lab
calculator.subtitle: All the calculations you need for knitting in one place. Enter your gauge and all tabs will calculate automatically.

patternConverter.title: Convert Pattern to My Gauge
patternConverter.description: Convert pattern stitches and rows to match your gauge when they differ.
patternConverter.solutionComfortable: Your gauge matches the pattern beautifully! You can safely knit with the recommended needle size.
patternConverter.solutionTight: Your fabric is tighter than the pattern. The finished width will be about {diff}cm SMALLER. We recommend using a larger needle (0.5mm~1.0mm UP) or knitting a size or two larger (L/XL).
patternConverter.solutionLoose: Your fabric is looser than the pattern. The finished width will be about {diff}cm LARGER. We recommend using a smaller needle (0.5mm~1.0mm DOWN) or knitting a size or two smaller (S/XS).

emptyState.title: Please enter your gauge first
emptyState.description: Enter your stitch and row count per 10cm in the bar above for accurate calculations.

sizeModal.title: Select Standard Size
sizeModal.disclaimer: Based on CYC standard measurements. Individual variations may apply.

basic.description: Convert length (cm) to stitches/rows, or stitches/rows to length (cm).

yarn.description: Calculate how many balls you need when substituting yarn from the original pattern.

requirement.totalLengthDesc: Enter the total length required for your project.

swatch.title: My Swatch (Sample) Info
swatch.desc: Enter the full width/height of the project you want to make.

raglan.description: Calculate cast-on and increase plan for a top-down raglan sweater.

sock.description: Enter foot circumference to calculate sock cast-on and heel flap stitches.
sock.negativeEaseDesc: Socks need to be 10% smaller than foot circumference to prevent slipping.

tips.title: Sock Knitting Tips
tips.tip2: Heel flap uses half of total stitches

sock.selectSizePlaceholder: Select a size or enter custom dimensions
sock.enterGaugeWarning: Please enter gauge to see results.

grading.descBasic: Scale pattern dimensions while maintaining original proportions.
grading.descAdvanced: Recalculate pattern based on your own gauge and target dimensions.
grading.myGaugeSubtitle: Recalculates pattern stitches based on your gauge input.

tutorials.title: byKnit User Guide
tutorials.subtitle: Discover how to master byKnit's core features to make your knitting faster and easier.

editor.title: Pattern Editor
editor.desc: Create your own digital patterns easily and quickly without graph paper.
editor.step1Desc: Enter the desired number of stitches and rows to create a new blank canvas.
editor.step2Desc: Select knitting or crochet symbols from the right toolbar and click anywhere on the canvas to fill your pattern.
editor.step3Desc: Your pattern saves automatically. When finished, click the download button at the top to export as PDF or PNG.

converter.title: Chart Converter
converter.desc: Upload a photo, and our AI automatically converts it into a colorwork knitting chart.
converter.step1Desc: Select and upload a photo or image you want to convert into a colorwork pattern.
converter.step2Desc: Set your desired number of stitches, rows, and maximum number of colors, then click convert.
converter.step3Desc: Review your converted chart. For fine-tuning, send it to the Pattern Editor to edit pixel by pixel.

calculator.title: Smart Calculator
calculator.desc: Leave the complex knitting math to our smart calculators.
calculator.step1Desc: When your gauge differs from the pattern, this tool automatically recalculates the required stitches and rows.
calculator.step2Desc: Calculate how many balls of yarn you'll need when using a different yarn weight or yardage than the original.
calculator.step3Desc: Easily scale your pattern to different sizes (e.g., from S to L) by calculating the necessary stitch adjustments.

translator.title: AI Translator
translator.desc: AI translates complex knitting abbreviations into natural language.
translator.step1Desc: Copy and paste the original pattern text into the left input box.
translator.step2Desc: Click translate, and the AI will interpret complex abbreviations like k, p, and k2tog into easy-to-read terms.
translator.step3Desc: Copy the translated pattern and save it to your personal notes or editor to use.

tutorials.placeholder: A demonstration video (GIF) for {title} will be placed here.
tutorials.footer: Have more questions? Please visit our community board or customer center.

community.title: Pattern Selling & Sharing
community.desc: Earn money by selling your patterns or share them for free with the knitting community.
community.step1Desc: Register your completed patterns from the editor to the Marketplace. Manage your sales easily in the Seller Dashboard.
community.step2Desc: Write a post in the Community board to share your free patterns, show off your projects, and exchange tips with others.
community.step3Desc: Receive secure and fast payouts from pattern sales through our integrated payment systems (Stripe, KakaoPay, etc).
